heading: Assistance and guidance in implementing programs
label: 9029 Assistance and guidance in implementing programs
notes: CodificationSection is comprised of . Subsec. (d) of repealed provisions formerly set out as a note under , Internal Revenue Code.section 2111 of Pub. L. 116–136section 2111 of Pub. L. 116–136section 3306 of Title 26

children.text: develop model legislative language, or disseminate existing model legislative language, which may be used by States in developing and enacting such programs, and periodically review and revise such model legislative language;
provide technical assistance and guidance in developing, enacting, and implementing such programs; and

children.label: A
children.text: the number of estimated averted layoffs;

children.label: B
children.text: the number of participating employers and workers; and

children.label: C
children.text: such other items as the Secretary of Labor determines are appropriate.

children.heading: Model language and guidance
children.label: b Model language and guidance
children.text: The model language and guidance developed under subsection (a) shall allow sufficient flexibility by States and participating employers while ensuring accountability and program integrity.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: c Consultation
children.text: In developing the model legislative language and guidance under subsection (a), and in order to meet the requirements of subsection (b), the Secretary shall consult with employers, labor organizations, State workforce agencies, and other program experts. Existing model legislative language that has been developed through such a consultative process shall be deemed to meet the consultation requirement of this subsection.
